---
title: "Assets & Licensing"
description: "How Sparta sources art and audio, and the one rule that decides what can ship."
---

The game runs **without any downloaded art** --- units draw as placeholder tokens in
`Unit.gd`. When real medieval art is wanted, Sparta uses genuinely free sources and
follows one hard rule about what may be bundled.

## The one rule: can we *bundle* it?

Anything committed under `assets/` is **redistributed to everyone who clones --- or
forks --- the repo**. So a source is usable as a bundled file only if its licence
permits **standalone redistribution of the (possibly modified) file**.

| | Meaning |
| :---: | --- |
| ✅ **bundle-safe** | CC0 / public domain --- zero obligations (we credit anyway). |
| 🅰 **bundle-safe, with attribution** | CC-BY / CC-BY-SA / OGA-BY --- commit, but keep the credit; `-SA` derivatives stay `-SA`. |
| ⛔ **reference-only** | `NC`, `ND`, or no standalone redistribution (Mixkit, Pixabay, BBC RemArc). Audition only --- never commit the file. |

::: {.callout-important}
## Being non-commercial doesn't loosen this --- it tightens it
The blocker is **redistribution**, not commerciality. Because forks inherit the repo
(code is MIT), anything carrying **`NC`** or **`ND`** is out: `NC` would bar
downstream/commercial forks, and `ND` forbids the routine adapting we do (trim,
resample, recolour, loop).

**Litmus test:** *"Could a stranger who forks our repo --- even to ship a commercial
game --- keep this exact file?"* CC0 / CC-BY / CC-BY-SA → yes. NC / ND / Mixkit /
Pixabay / BBC RemArc → no.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Do not use Total War mod assets
They are **not** public domain --- modders retain copyright and many derive from
copyrighted base-game art. Shipping them is legally risky. Stick to the CC0 sources
below.
:::

## Recommended CC0 graphics

| Pack | Use for | Licence | Link |
| --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| Toen's Medieval Strategy Sprite Pack (16×16) | Soldiers, cavalry, siege, banners | CC0 | [OpenGameArt](https://opengameart.org/content/toens-medieval-strategy-sprite-pack-v10-16x16) |
| Kenney --- game assets | UI buttons, panels, fonts, tiles | CC0 | [kenney.nl](https://kenney.nl/assets) |
| OpenGameArt --- CC0 collection | Terrain, grass/dirt tiles, props | CC0 (verify each) | [OpenGameArt search](https://opengameart.org/art-search-advanced?keys=&field_art_licenses_tid%5B%5D=4) |
| game-icons.net | Ability / unit / status icons (4000+) | CC-BY 3.0 🅰 | [game-icons.net](https://game-icons.net/) |

## How to add art

1. Download a pack and unzip it.
2. Drop the PNGs into `assets/sprites/` (units) or `assets/ui/` (interface).
3. Godot auto-imports them when the editor regains focus.
4. Wire a `Sprite2D` into `Unit.gd._ready()` --- see the README's *"Swapping
   placeholder art"* section.

## Audio

Sparta currently ships a CC0 sound set sourced from **OpenGameArt** (sword clash,
swishes, thwack, trumpet, fanfare for hit/shoot/rout/death/select/order/victory/
defeat). Per-file sources and licences are recorded in
[`assets/sfx/CREDITS.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/assets/sfx/CREDITS.md).

## Currently bundled

- **Audio:** OpenGameArt CC0 set (see the credits file above).
- **Graphics:** none --- procedural placeholder primitives in `Unit.gd`.

::: {.callout-note}
## Code vs assets
The project's **code** is [MIT-licensed](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/LICENSE).
Bundled **assets** keep their own licences (CC0 / CC-BY), recorded in the receipts.
MIT never implies a licence over the third-party art and audio.
:::

---

*Full running catalog (audio + graphics, including reference-only sources) lives in
[`docs/asset-sources.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/docs/asset-sources.md);
the standing art shortlist is in
[`ASSETS.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/ASSETS.md).*
